quick_facts.label: Service and cadre
quick_facts.value: Indian Radio Regulatory Service (IRRS) is a Group ‘A’ cadre of the Department of Telecommunications (DoT), associated with the Wireless Planning & Coordination (WPC) Wing and its field organization, the Wireless Monitoring Organization (WMO).

quick_facts.label: Core public function
quick_facts.value: WPC is India’s national radio-regulatory nodal agency and is responsible for planning, engineering, regulating, managing, and monitoring radio-frequency spectrum.

quick_facts.label: Usual direct-recruitment route
quick_facts.value: IRRS Group ‘A’ has been listed by UPSC under the Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering category of the Engineering Services Examination (ESE); actual vacancies, eligibility, and service allocation depend on the relevant annual UPSC notification and rules.

quick_facts.label: Operational footprint
quick_facts.value: The WMO performs wireless monitoring through a network reported by DoT’s capacity-building documentation as one International Satellite Monitoring Earth Station, five International Monitoring Stations, and 22 Wireless Monitoring Stations across India.

quick_facts.label: Training
quick_facts.value: National Communications Academy–Wireless (NCA-W) provides induction training for newly recruited Group ‘A’ and ‘B’ IRRS probationers and technical training for WPC/WMO personnel.

quick_facts.label: Cadre structure
quick_facts.value: The May 2024 cadre review recorded IRRS Group ‘A’ Junior Time Scale at Pay Matrix Level 10, with higher grades through Senior Time Scale, Junior Administrative Grade, Senior Administrative Grade, and Higher Administrative Grade.

sections.heading: What an IRRS officer does
paragraphs: IRRS officers work in the WPC/WMO system that manages and monitors the radio-frequency spectrum. In practice, this combines technical spectrum-management work with administration of radio authorizations and regulatory processes: WPC is responsible for the planning, engineering, regulation, management, and monitoring of RF spectrum, while WMO is the field unit that conducts wireless monitoring.

WPC’s licensing and assignment work spans terrestrial and satellite radio uses. For example, DoT states that WPC grants frequency assignments for satellite-based telecommunications and broadcasting networks, and the Unified Licence framework requires separate WPC frequency allotment and Wireless Operating Licences for wireless elements of telecom services.
bullets: Analyse spectrum-use proposals and support frequency assignment, licensing, and related regulatory decisions.
Conduct or supervise radio-monitoring work through WMO facilities, helping identify and address unauthorized or non-compliant wireless emissions.
Handle technically detailed submissions such as link budgets, carrier plans, RF-chain diagrams, equipment information, and applicable clearance or authorization workflows, depending on the service.
Contribute to national spectrum-management work and, in the WPC context, matters involving satellite orbit and spectrum coordination.

sections.heading: Entry through UPSC Engineering Services Examination
paragraphs: The canonical direct-entry route is the UPSC Engineering Services Examination (ESE/IES). UPSC’s 2025 ESE notice included Indian Radio Regulatory Service Group ‘A’ in the Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering service category. UPSC’s examination archive shows that ESE is notified on an annual-cycle basis; the 2026 preliminary examination, for example, was notified on September 26, 2025 and held on February 8, 2026.

A candidate should treat the live UPSC ESE notification—not an older service list—as controlling. The annual notice specifies the available IRRS vacancies, age and educational eligibility, examination stages, medical standards, reservation, preferences, and final service-allocation conditions.
bullets: Build eligibility around the engineering discipline and qualification requirements stated in the applicable UPSC ESE notice.
Prepare for the ESE examination stages and the Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering papers where applicable.
Track the annual notification closely: inclusion of IRRS and the number of vacancies can vary by examination year.
After selection and allocation, complete the probationary induction training arranged for IRRS officers by NCA-W.

sections.heading: Knowledge and skills that fit the role
paragraphs: The role is best suited to engineers who can apply electronics, telecommunications, radio-frequency, and satellite-communication knowledge in a regulatory setting. The documentation and workflows used for WPC authorizations illustrate the need to interpret technical evidence alongside rules, procedures, and service conditions.

Because WPC/WMO work includes spectrum monitoring and service authorization, officers also need careful written analysis, data and document review, compliance-oriented judgement, and the ability to communicate technical requirements to applicants, operators, and government stakeholders.
bullets: Radio propagation, RF systems, antennas, spectrum planning, interference analysis, and wireless-network fundamentals.
Electronics and telecommunications engineering, with sufficient familiarity with satellite and terrestrial communication systems.
Ability to assess technical documents such as link budgets, carrier plans, and RF-chain diagrams.

Working knowledge of telecom, spectrum, licensing, and international radio-regulation frameworks; DoT training material identifies DoT, TRAI, and ITU-related rules and policies as important domain competencies.
Clear technical writing and disciplined application of procedures, conditions, fees, and compliance requirements.

sections.heading: Where and how the work is performed
paragraphs: IRRS is a central-government technical service within DoT’s WPC/WMO ecosystem. Work can combine policy, coordination, licensing, and technical scrutiny with field-oriented monitoring functions. DoT describes WMO as a WPC field unit and reports monitoring infrastructure distributed across the country, so postings are not limited to a single headquarters office.

The organization also has regional licensing and monitoring facilities. DoT’s e-services information identifies regional WPC offices in the North, East, West, South, and North-East, and its wireless training centre is located at the IMS campus at Ghitorni, New Delhi.
bullets: Desk-based work may include spectrum-assignment cases, authorization scrutiny, policy implementation, records, and stakeholder correspondence.
Technical and field work may involve radio-monitoring stations, measurement and monitoring systems, inspections, and coordination with regional offices.
Assignments may expose officers to telecom, broadcasting, satellite, maritime, aviation, experimental, and other wireless-use cases.

sections.heading: Career progression
paragraphs: The 2024 IRRS Group ‘A’ cadre review provides the clearest official current framework for progression: Junior Time Scale (JTS) is Pay Matrix Level 10; Senior Time Scale is Level 11/12; Junior Administrative Grade is Level 13; Senior Administrative Grade is Level 14; and Higher Administrative Grade is Level 15. Advancement is governed by the applicable cadre, service, promotion, vigilance, and government personnel rules rather than being automatic on a fixed individual timetable.

Published DoT seniority material also shows working designations at higher levels, including Assistant Wireless Adviser/Engineer-in-Charge at Level 11 and Deputy Wireless Adviser/Deputy Director at Level 12. Actual duties and postings evolve with grade and organizational requirement.
bullets: Entry grade: Junior Time Scale (JTS), Pay Matrix Level 10.
Middle grades: Senior Time Scale and Junior Administrative Grade, with progressively larger technical, supervisory, and administrative responsibilities.
Senior grades: Senior Administrative Grade and Higher Administrative Grade, subject to cadre structure and applicable appointment processes.
Career development includes induction and continuing technical training through NCA-W.

sections.heading: Compensation context
paragraphs: Compensation is determined under Central Government pay rules, the officer’s appointed grade, admissible allowances, location, and later promotions; it should not be treated as a single fixed salary. As of the May 2024 IRRS cadre-review notification, the entry JTS grade is mapped to Pay Matrix Level 10, while the service’s senior grades extend through Level 15.

For context on later grades, a DoT provisional seniority list dated January 1, 2025 identifies Assistant Wireless Adviser/Engineer-in-Charge at Level 11 and Deputy Wireless Adviser/Deputy Director at Level 12. Pay, allowances, and revisions can change, so candidates should verify the current central-government pay instructions and the appointment offer for their own year.
bullets: Entry context: JTS, Pay Matrix Level 10, according to the 2024 IRRS cadre review.
Progression context: higher IRRS grades are mapped through Levels 11/12, 13, 14, and 15 in that cadre-review document.
Do not use an advertised coaching-site salary figure as definitive: starting pay in hand depends on current pay orders, allowances, deductions, and posting location.
